name: On The Morning You Wake (To the End of The World)

reviews.review_description: Shows that VR is for more than just gaming...  Beautiful presentation, sure it gets a bit preachy, but the subject matter kind of demands that....

Attention seeking narzism App from a trivial story.
HEY IT WAS JUST A FAIL ALARM!!!!  Nothing happens at the end! omg 😝

Loved this important immersive and unusual experience. Very interesting merging of an important global issue but made from a local cultural stance. Immersive experiences like these are a good way to make us look and think about these important global issues. Well done!
Insipid

Pros: +Good visuals............

+Interesting story on the 2018 Hawaii false missile alert............

Cons: -Heavy-handed on the angsty emotional content, to a fault.......

-Conclusion is weak and naive...........


If you are looking for a nuclear bomb explosion simulator, keep moving along, this ain't it. If you are looking for an angsty, Hawaiian take on the 2018 Hawaii false missile alert, you have found the right place.

It is only my personal opinion that the developers fail to conclude their final argument with any kind of strength because they went overboard with personal emotion, and portions where they stopped speaking English and spoke in a Hawaiian island dialect instead(without subtitles). If you are looking to change the world and have everyone scrap their nuclear weapons, you are going to have to bring much stronger rationale than what you have prepared here. Also, I did not care for the developers attempt to redefine what an act of violence is. Just to remind you, possessing a nuclear weapon for deterrent purposes is in itself -not- an act of violence, according to the global definition of it.
I thought you could interact with the story but no

I got bored 
  I wanted to do something 
Maybe you could as goes on

But I got bored quickly
Haven’t even played yet but excited to play because of the reviews hope it doesn’t let me down
Not what I was expecting from an end of the world experience.

Wonderful experience. It’s an incredible documentary with top notch audio and visual experience.

Suggestion: hand gesture implementation
Moving piece of work, perfect execution of sound and visuals ..

I am from Hawai’i and had to live through this experience.  This app is a treasure and also allows many of us to connect to the ontology of cultural practice as well as comprehension of what Native Hawaiians living in the islands face regarding issues such as colonialism and neocolonialism and settler colonialism.  The art style is superb and very moving.

An incredible experience to begin with, looking at a Hawaiian sunset and seeing incredible visuals. There’s no actual interaction in this three-parter besides picking up a virtual cell phone a few times, so it’s a cinematic experience. The first part accurately portrays and covers the fear Hawaii went through receiving the missile strike warning. The second was an advocacy against nuclear weapons and armament. The third part was very short and degenerated into partisan activism, and bugs out after a hand-holding scene into a still image that follows your vision, requiring you to manually close the app to get out. They actually allowed one person to infer in the presentation that we wouldn’t have conflict if non-male, non-white people were making the decisions. I understand there’s going to be bigotry from some of the indigenous people there, but it was deeply unprofessional and offensive to allow it to be aired in this presentation. The message other than the bigotry is positive and it was a beautiful visual experience, but it was promptly deleted after because no one should have to endure or ignore racism to appreciate a product.

I really could have done without all the mythological nonsense and attempt at being poetic and dramatic.  I had to silence the audio because it was so full of obscure Hawaiian mythological terminology. I had no idea what the heck the narrator was talking about and neither will you unless you're well schooled in Hawaiian mythology.  Why couldn't they just explain what happened without all the superstitious nonsense and dramatic talking?  The graphics were great though.
The game is just crappy 360 videos
After loading it in the Oculus 2, each segment only plays for about 1 minute before returning to the start screen.

reviews.review_description: Wow - still processing. The artistry and tapestry of stories juxtaposed against the horror these poor souls must of have felt can't help but make one rethink all we know supposedly about nuclear threat.
1 to 3 star raters deserve to be nuked in their as$ lol....

Saw the title, was intrigued. Life after a nuclear attack?? How will I survive?? NOPE, this is just a three part poorly animated documentary, no game play involved. While the topic is obviously relevant I sat through 45 mins of repetitive narrative waiting for the game to start. Could have watched this on PBS for free. $29.00??? Refund requested. A complete rip off....

reviews.review_description: Amazing graphics and well done
I want to talk with my mom after watching it ;-;

Visuals are good and so is the storytelling, but it kinda drags on. If you’ve heard the story already this isn’t really worth getting, there’s even a part where some lady says a prayer in a foreign language for like 45 seconds for no reason, I didn’t even finish it because it took way too long

reviews.review_title: Beautiful representation for a cold mome
reviews.review_description: My wife and I live Oahu and I remember that morning , getting ready for the day, getting the message and then looking at my wife who was asleep. I didn’t want wake her in a panic, I suppose I just wanted to go out peacefully. Hints the 4 out of 5 , I really enjoyed the Hawaiian history and folklore (for a lack of better terms, a part of me really believes in “aloha and mana “ philosophy they preach here) and….. would really love to see more of that type of content in the future !

Someone commented something like “if you heard the story, then you don’t need this.” On the contrary, if you heard the story, it was probably through a short-lived news cycle and otherwise through a series of memes and other jokes about it. 

Put aside your thoughts on activism and politics, and tune in to see, by way of first-hand accounts, how unprepared, helpless and hopeless things will be once it really hits fan. For example, you hear from a father and his kids who were turned away from shelter- just as you’d see in any apocalypse movie and think how absurd that is. It’s amazing to hear from the people went through that, and to know that they had to face what for them was their absolute doom- and, of course, it was a false alarm but they now have to live with that experience. 

The first hand accounts were not something I had really dug into previously. The emotional impact for something that didn’t happen was unexpected. And I think that’s the point. Well made, and my opinion says it’s the best (most impactful) free experience on quest.
Awesome job. Lovely special effects. Also a very good message and scary situation.

Wow this was an experience of fine story telling and excellent VR artistry.
I just thought I would check it out late last night, just to see if I would watch it later.
Watched it all and will more then likely watch again.
I am not certain but I think it lasts the 38 minutes, made by 38 minutes production.
That how long these people thought it was the end. 
Highly recommended and it's free.
It does not load

developer_response.body:
Hello spirit7753, thanks for the feedback and sorry for the inconvenience!
Our VR experience should be able to load with or without WiFi connection, you can try deleting and reinstalling it on your device.
If you still have trouble, please contact us at storemanager@artefrance.fr (screenshots, details about how you get to this bug, etc).
Have a great day!

reviews.review_description: This story really brought me there with the people of Hawaii and what they experienced with the false alarm.  I remember hearing about it on the news, but I didn’t realize the impact until I went through this VR experience.
Beyond gaming, this experience is where VR really shines. Aesthetically wonderful and the message is strong, Bravo!
A creative use of VR for story telling that puts you inside the events. Gripping!
Well made!
Very good statement against nuclear weapons in general!
Really enjoyed this experience, great way to highlight the violence of nuclear war and a terrible terrifying experience the islanders must of experienced. Well worth a watch.
You must experience this…
Nice choice on store

Once you add other objects to be scan to you room they can't be removed, and the game will be giving you an 404 error to start the next time. The roomscale will not communicate with the game and now the other objects are unable to be removed otherwise than the table/couch.
It's a VR movie about the false alarm for a ballistic missile, that was sent in Hawaii in 2018. I liked the artistic interpretation and the display of what is possible with the Quest 2.
I was abled to install and run this on the Quest 1 even as it says not compatible with Quest 1.
